etymology.description: 时(時) (shí, "time; season") + 刻(刻) (kè, "moment; quarter hour") = specific points in time or instances
etymology.interpretation: 时(時) (shí, "time") represents the general concept of time or duration, while 刻(刻) (kè, "moment") denotes a precise, smaller unit of time like a 'moment' or 'quarter hour.' Together, they refer to specific points or instances in time, such as an 'hour' or 'occasion' when particular events or actions occur.

examples.chinese: 在关键时刻(在關鍵時刻)，他总能保持冷静(他總能保持冷靜)。
examples.pinyin: Zài guānjiàn shíkè, tā zǒng néng bǎochí lěngjìng.
examples.english: At critical moments, he always remains calm.

examples.chinese: 我们需要珍惜每一个时刻(我們需要珍惜每一個時刻)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen xūyào zhēnxī měi yī gè shíkè.
examples.english: We need to cherish every moment.

examples.chinese: 她在正确的时刻出现(她在正確的時刻出現)，拯救了大家(拯救了大家)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài zhèngquè de shíkè chūxiàn, zhěngjiùle dàjiā.
examples.english: She appeared at the right moment and saved everyone.
